quiz.title: Midweek Quiz: Units 1 and 2
quiz.description: This quiz checks understanding of the course material from Days 1 through 5, including ocean foundations, phytoplankton, zooplankton, and marine food-web processes.

questions.question:
Which statement best explains why the ocean is central to Earth systems?
It regulates climate, stores heat, and supports global biogeochemical cycles. It affects only coastal weather and has little influence inland. It matters mainly as a transportation route for human activity. It is biologically important only in tropical regions.

Match each seafloor feature with the best description.
Open-water surface current driven only by tides Photosynthetic zone limited to estuaries
Cold, salty seawater is generally denser than warm, less saline seawater.

Which factors are directly important to seawater chemistry and marine productivity?
Dissolved gases Nutrient availability Temperature-driven density structure Only moon phase, with no role for chemistry
The downward transport of carbon from surface waters to deeper ocean layers is called the [blank].

Why are phytoplankton ecologically important?
They form the base of many marine food webs through primary production. They are the largest marine predators in coastal ecosystems. They prevent nutrient cycling by removing all dissolved gases. They live only on the seafloor near hydrothermal vents.
In three to five sentences, explain how zooplankton connect surface primary production to higher trophic levels and to carbon export into deeper water.
